task_completion.description: Access the Wazuh manager configuration file
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a command to edit /var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf (e.g., using vi/nano via docker exec) OR the Wazuh web interface configuration editor open.
An <integration> XML block is visible in the configuration file containing <name>virustotal</name>.
The <api_key> tag inside the integration block contains exactly '9c3a5b8e2f1d4a7c6b0e9f8d7c5a3b1e4f2d6a8c0b9e7f5d3a1c4b6e8f0a2d74'.

task_completion.description: Configure the rule group, minimum alert level, and alert format
Restart the Wazuh manager to apply changes
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a command like 'docker restart wazuh-wazuh.manager-1' or 'systemctl restart wazuh-manager' inside the container, or a successful restart confirmation in the Wazuh web interface.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Wazuh container or web interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a real terminal executing docker commands or the official Wazuh web UI, not a local text editor mocking the file.

integrity.description: Configuration is applied to the correct container
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal prompt, docker command, or UI context clearly indicates interaction with 'wazuh-wazuh.manager-1' and not a local host file.
The restart command shows standard output confirming execution (e.g., the container name echoed back by Docker), rather than just being typed into the prompt without pressing enter.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target configuration file is /var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf in the wazuh-wazuh.manager-1 container. The integration block must contain: <name>virustotal</name>, <api_key>9c3a5b8e2f1d4a7c6b0e9f8d7c5a3b1e4f2d6a8c0b9e7f5d3a1c4b6e8f0a2d74</api_key>, <group>syscheck</group>, <level>7</level>, and <alert_format>json</alert_format>.
